user_query:
I run Indigenous cultural awareness trainings and need an event-triggered automation that gathers official land/territory acknowledgement guidance for new training locations. Please implement a local file-watcher that monitors a newline-delimited JSON ledger and, when a new event is appended, performs an Internet search and writes structured results.

Inputs and where to use them:
- input/events_schema.json: JSON Schema all event lines must satisfy.
- input/events_seed.jsonl: seed data to initialize the ledger.
- input/cities_reference.csv: optional query hints by city; if a matching city is present, incorporate its query_terms, otherwise default to "Indigenous land acknowledgement CITY COUNTRY".
- input/new_event.json: a valid event you should append during testing to trigger the automation.

Required behavior and outputs:
1) Initialize a ledger at workspace/events.jsonl.
   - If workspace/events.jsonl does not exist, create it by copying input/events_seed.jsonl as-is.
2) Provide an executable watcher at automation/watch_events.py that runs locally and monitors workspace/events.jsonl for appended lines (polling or FS events is fine).
   - On each new line, parse JSON and validate against input/events_schema.json. If invalid, write a single-line entry with the event id (if present), timestamp, and validation error to logs/invalid_events.log and skip web search.
   - If valid, perform live search engine queries from the Internet using a general-purpose search engine.
     • Build the query: if the event city appears in input/cities_reference.csv (case-insensitive), use that row’s query_terms; otherwise use: "Indigenous land acknowledgement" + city + country.
     • Retrieve at least 3 and at most 10 unique results. For each result, capture title and URL. Classify each result into domain_type: government (.gov or contains .gov.), education (.edu), nonprofit (.org), or other.
   - Write a JSON artifact at output/search_results/<event_id>-<city_slug>.json with fields:
     {
       "event_id": string,
       "city": string,
       "query": string,
       "retrieved_at": ISO8601 string,
       "results": [ { "title": string, "url": string, "domain_type": "government"|"education"|"nonprofit"|"other" } ]
     }
     where city_slug is the lowercase city name with spaces replaced by dashes. Ensure at least 3 results are present.
   - Maintain a cumulative CSV index at output/summary/links_index.csv (create if absent) with columns:
     event_id,city,total_results,gov_count,edu_count,org_count,other_count. Append or update the row for the event.
3) Provide a test/validation script run_tests.sh that can be executed locally. It must:
   - Seed workspace/events.jsonl from input/events_seed.jsonl if needed.
   - Start the watcher, append input/new_event.json (as a single line) to workspace/events.jsonl, and wait up to 60 seconds for the corresponding output/search_results/<event_id>-<city_slug>.json to appear.
   - Validate that every line in workspace/events.jsonl conforms to input/events_schema.json (exit non-zero if any line fails).
   - Validate that the produced output JSON contains the specified top-level fields and at least 3 results with http(s) URLs and recognized domain_type values; also validate that output/summary/links_index.csv has a row for the new event with correct counts summing to total_results.
   - Stop the watcher cleanly and exit 0 on success; exit non-zero on failures.

Notes and constraints:
- Use only local files, scripts, and shell commands. The watcher must perform real Internet search queries to populate results. Do not hardcode content or URLs; rely on live search. Manual browsing is not acceptable.
- Keep the project self-contained; do not require external credentials or special infrastructure.
- Artifacts that will be checked: logs/invalid_events.log (if any invalid input occurs), output/search_results/*.json for new events, and output/summary/links_index.csv. The evaluator will run: bash run_tests.sh

Deliverables expected under the workspace:
- automation/watch_events.py (the watcher, may use any libraries but must be runnable locally)
- run_tests.sh (executable test/validation script per above)
- Any small helper modules/scripts you create must live under automation/ or scripts/

Please implement this so that it’s robust enough for my training workflow and passes the validation in run_tests.sh. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
